title: ficus gibbosa var. parasitica
intro: Ficus gibbosa var. parasitica, also known as the "strangling fig," is a plant species that is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It is a type of hemi-epiphyte, which means that it grows on other plants while also developing its own roots and eventually killing the host plant. The medicinal properties of Ficus gibbosa var. parasitica include its ability to lower blood pressure and reduce inflammation due to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions that are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant.
